pattern: A binary matrix with very few columns only has a handful of distinct row patterns, so whenever any workable subset of rows exists, one made of a single all-zero row or a pair of bit-disjoint rows already works.
hints: There are at most 2^n distinct row patterns since each column only ever holds a 0 or a 1 - two rows that look identical are the same pattern for this purpose.
A single row is a good subset exactly when it is all zeros, and a pair of rows is a good subset exactly when no column has a 1 in both of them at once.

Because the column count is so small, dedupe the rows by pattern, look for an all-zero pattern first, and otherwise test every pair of distinct patterns for having no shared 1-bit - you never need to consider larger subsets.

constraints.note: every row has the same length n, where 1 <= n <= 5

edge_inputs.description: a single all-zero row is trivially good on its own
a single non-zero row has no good subset
two rows whose 1-bits never overlap form a good pair
two identical non-zero rows always share a 1-bit, so no pair works
the only all-zero row is buried behind several identical non-zero rows
a single-column matrix still resolves via the first zero row
maximum column count with every row identical and non-zero leaves no good subset
the first candidate pair overlaps but a later pair of patterns is disjoint

stress.description: near-maximum row count with a random column width and random bits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, size)
    n = rng.randint(1, 5)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]

stress.description: large row count drawn from a small pool of repeated patterns at the maximum column width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, size)
    n = 5
    pool = [rng.randint(0, (1 << n) - 1) for _ in range(6)]
    grid = []
    for _ in range(m):
        p = rng.choice(pool)
        grid.append([(p >> j) & 1 for j in range(n)])
    return [grid]

complexity.time: O(m * n + 2^n * n) — at most 2^n <= 32 distinct row patterns for n <= 5
